LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HUYGHUE'S RENEWED MOTION FOR CHARGING ORDER AND ASSIGNMENT ORDER - THE COURT DISCLOSES THAT ALTHOUGH SHE SHARES THE SAME LAST NAME AS COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT, SHE IS NOT RELATED. FURTHER, THE COURT DISCLOSES HER PRIOR EMPLOYMENT WITH HOWARD RICE (AND WORK WITH HAMBRECHT & QUIST CIRCA 1983). AS TO THE MOTION FOR A CHARGING AND ASSIGNMENT ORDER WITH RESPECT TO WR HAMBRECHT/PRIVATE EQUITY INVESTMENTS, LLC, WR HAMBRECHT VENTURES II MANAGEMENT, LLC, HAMBRECHT SUMA CAPITAL I MANAGEMENT, LLC, WR HAMBRECHT/ARISTOTLE 2 MANAGEMENT, LLC, WR HAMBRECHT/ARISTOTLE MANAGEMENT, LLC, WR HAMBRECHT/ODYSSEY, LLC, WR HAMBRECHT/TWITTER, AND GRANITE VENTURES, LLC, THE MATTER IS CONTINUED FOR FURTHER HEARING TO APRIL 18, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. DEFENDANT MAY RESPOND TO THE NEW EVIDENCE PLAINTIFF PRESENTED BY APRIL 9, 2013. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE REPLY NO LATER APRIL 12, 2013. AS TO THE MOTION FOR A CHARGING AND ASSIGNMENT ORDER WITH RESPECT TO H&Q ARIAT INVESTMENT MGMT. CO., LLC, AND W.R. HAMBRECHT/VIZU, LLC, THE COURT CONTINUES MOTION FOR FURTHER HEARING TO APRIL 18, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302 SO DEFENDANT MAY PRESENT MORE FACTS ABOUT THE CHRONOLOGY OF THE LIQUIDATIONS. THE MOTION FOR A CHARGING AND ASSIGNMENT ORDER WITH RESPECT TO ALL OTHER LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANIES, THE THREE LIMITED PARTNERSHIPS, BOTH BANK ACCOUNTS, AND THE REAL PROPERTY RENTAL IS UNOPPOSED AND THEREFORE GRANTED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-26-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HUYGHUE NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR DEFAULT AND TO COMPEL COMPLIANCE WITH LEVY, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO ESTABLISH LIABILITY IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PURSUANT TO DEFENDANT'S CONSENT AND REQUEST FOR SERVICE ON COUNSEL, FILED ON AUGUST 31, 2012, SERVICE OF ALL PLEADINGS, WRITS, NOTICES, ORDERS AND OTHER PAPERS ARE TO BE SERVED ON DEFENDANT WILLIAM R. HAMBRECHT'S COUNSEL. (SEE CCP 684.020(B).) THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT THIS REQUEST HAS BEEN REVOKED BY DEFENDANT OR THAT CONSENT HAS BEEN REVOKED BY HIS ATTORNEYS. (SEE CCP 684.020(B)(2), (3).) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PROVIDED EVIDENCE THAT THE REQUIRED LEVY DOCUMENTS UNDER CCP 700.101 WERE SERVED ON DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL. THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT THEY WERE SERVED ON THE SERVICE AGENT OF W.R. HAMBRECHT & CO. THIS IS INSUFFICIENT SERVICE UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ARLENE HOWELL CSR2542.

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HUYGHUE'S RENEWED MOTION FOR CHARGING ORDER AND ASSIGNMENT ORDER IS GRANTED WITH RESPECT TO H&Q ARIAT INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT, LLC AND W.R. HAMBRECHT/VIZU, LLC. TO THE EXTENT THAT FUNDS REMAIN WITH THESE TWO LIQUIDATED LLCS, PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO ANY DISTRIBUTIONS TO DEFENDANT, IF ANY, THAT MAY OCCUR. THE MOTION FOR A CHARGING AND ASSIGNMENT ORDER IS GRANTED AS TO W.R. HAMBRECHT VENTURES II MANAGEMENT, LLC. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANT'S SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION CORRECTS HIS EARLIER DEPOSITION TESTIMONY THAT HE IS NOT A MANAGER OF THIS ENTITY, THE ISSUANCE OF THE CHARGING ORDER WILL NOT HARM OR PREJUDICE DEFENDANT OR THE LLC. IF THERE ARE DISTRIBUTIONS MADE TO DEFENDANT FROM THIS LLC, THEY WILL BE DISTRIBUTED TO JUDGMENT CREDITOR; IF THERE ARE NONE BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS NO INTEREST IN THIS LLC, THERE WILL BE NO RESULTING PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT OR THE ENTITY THE MOTION FOR A CHARGING AND ASSIGNMENT ORDER IS DENIED AS TO THE REMAINING ENTITIES BECAUSE PLAINTIFF APPEARS TO HAVE WITHDRAWN ITS REQUEST FOR A CHARGING AND ASSIGNMENT ORDER WITH RESPECT TO THE OTHER FIVE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANIES THAT ARE THE SUBJECT OF THIS MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HUYGHUE'S MOTION TO FILE DOCUMENTS UNDER SEAL IS GRANTED, FOR GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED AND RELATES TO ACCOUNT NAMES AND NUMBERS AND FINANCIAL INFORMATION DESIGNATED AS CONFIDENTIAL BY THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR. NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST OF THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR IN PROTECTING THIS CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. A REDACTED VERSION OF THE DOCUMENTS WILL BE AVAILABLE IN THE COURT'S PUBLIC FILES. THE MOVING PARTY IS REQUESTED TO PREPARE AN ORDER ADDING THIS LANGUAGE TO THE PROPOSED ORDER TO FILE DOCUMENTS UNDER SEAL SUBMITTED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS AND TO PRESENT IT TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ARLENE HOWELL CSR2542.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default And To Compel Compliance With Levy, Or In The Alternative To Establish Liability; Memorandum Points And Authorities ---- Redacted
Off Calendar Aug-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: BENJAMIN MORRIS, ESQ., 619.234.6655, FOR PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HUYGHUE.
DENNIS MILLER, ESQ., 415.981.0550, FOR DEFENDANT WILLIAM HAMBRECHT.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HUYGHUE Notice Of Motion And Motion For Default And To Compel Compliance With Levy, Or In The Alternative To Establish Liability IS Denied without prejudice. Pursuant to Defendant's Consent and Request for Service on Counsel, filed on August 31, 2012, service of all pleadings, writs, notices, orders and other papers are to be served on defendant William R. Hambrecht's counsel. (See CCP 684.020(b).) There is no evidence that this request has been revoked by Defendant or that consent has been revoked by his attorneys. (See CCP 684.020(b)(2), (3).) Plaintiff has not provided evidence that the required levy documents under CCP 700.101 were served on Defendant's counsel. The evidence shows that they were served on the service agent of W.R. Hambrecht & Co. This is insufficient service under the circumstances. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR2542.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Documents Under Seal
Off Calendar Aug-02-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: BENJAMIN MORRIS, ESQ., 619.234.6655, FOR PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HUYGHUE.
DENNIS MILLER, ESQ., 415.981.0550, FOR DEFENDANT WILLIAM HAMBRECHT.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HUYGHUE'S Motion To File Documents Under Seal IS Granted, for good cause shown. No opposition filed. The Court finds that the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored and relates to account names and numbers and financial information designated as confidential by the judgment debtor. No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest of the judgment debtor in protecting this confidential information. A redacted version of the documents will be available in the Court's public files. The moving party is requested to prepare an order adding this language to the proposed Order to File Documents Under Seal submitted with the moving papers and to present it to the Court for signature. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR2542.

Ntc Of Renewed Mtn For Charging Order And Assigment Order
Off Calendar Apr-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter.

Judge Miller discloses that she was formerly a partner at Morrison & Foerster LLP from 1997 until she was appointed to the bench in March 2005. Judge Miller noticed on the deposition excerpt submitted by Mr. Hambrecht in the supplemental briefing on April 9, 2013 that he was defended at a deposition on February 26, 2013 by an attorney from the New York office of Morrison & Foerster. Judge Miller does not know this attorney. Judge Miller does not believe that this affects her ability to be fair and impartial in this case.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Michael Huyghue's Renewed Motion for charging order and assignment order is granted with respect to H&Q Ariat Investment Management, LLC and W.R. Hambrecht/VIZU, LLC. To the extent that funds remain with these two liquidated LLCs, Plaintiff is entitled to any distributions to Defendant, if any, that may occur. The motion for a charging and assignment order is granted as to W.R. Hambrecht Ventures II Management, LLC. Although Defendant's supplemental declaration corrects his earlier deposition testimony that he is not a manager of this entity, the issuance of the charging order will not harm or prejudice Defendant or the LLC. If there are distributions made to Defendant from this LLC, they will be distributed to judgment creditor; if there are none because Defendant has no interest in this LLC, there will be no resulting prejudice to Defendant or the entity The motion for a charging and assignment order is denied as to the remaining entities because Plaintiff appears to have withdrawn its request for a charging and assignment order with respect to the other five limited liability companies that are the subject of this motion. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Ntc Of Renewed Mtn For Charging Order And Assigment Order
Off Calendar Mar-26-2013 Continued To Apr-18-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: benjamin morris, esq., 619.234.6655, for plaintiff michael huyghue.
dennis miller, esq., 415.981.0550, for defendant william hambrecht.

the court discloses that although she shares the same last name as counsel for defendant, she is not related. Further, the Court discloses her prior employment with Howard Rice (and work with Hambrecht & quist circa 1983).

RULING: matter discussed. at conclusion of discussion, the Court adopted its tentative ruling regarding PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HUYGHUE'S Renewed Motion For Charging Order And Assignment Order.

As to the motion for a charging and assignment order with respect to WR Hambrecht/Private Equity Investments, LLC, WR Hambrecht Ventures II Management, LLC, Hambrecht Suma Capital I Management, LLC, WR Hambrecht/Aristotle 2 Management, LLC, WR Hambrecht/Aristotle Management, LLC, WR Hambrecht/Odyssey, LLC, WR Hambrecht/Twitter, and Granite Ventures, LLC, the matter is continued for further hearing to april 18, 2013 at 9:30 am in department 302. Defendant may respond to the new evidence Plaintiff presented by April 9, 2013. Plaintiff may file reply no later April 12, 2013.

As to the motion for a charging and assignment order with respect to H&Q Ariat Investment Mgmt. Co., LLC, and W.R. Hambrecht/VIZU, LLC, the Court continues motion for further hearing to april 18, 2013 at 9:30 am in department 302 so defendant may present more facts about the chronology of the liquidations.

The motion for a charging and assignment order with respect to all other limited liability companies, the three limited partnerships, both bank accounts, and the real property rental is unopposed and therefore granted. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
